meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Freelancers: Beat Developer Burnout
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Use this intermediate checklist to ship faster, impress clients, and master project management using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To stop abandoning projects, transition from 'coding mode' to 'product management mode.' By leveraging Claude Code for rapid implementation and Antigravity for structural integrity, you break complex features into atomic, shippable units. This systematic approach ensures you maintain momentum, hit every client deadline, and finish what you start.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished side projects is paved with good intentions and scope creep. You start with a burst of adrenaline, build the core architecture, and then hit the 'messy middle'—where bugs multiply, requirements shift, and the initial vision fades into a pile of unmerged branches. It is the silent killer of every freelance career.

But the best builders don't rely on willpower; they rely on systems. By treating your freelance builds like professional product deployments, you remove the guesswork. We are moving from chaotic hacking to high-velocity shipping using a rigorous project management stack that turns your IDE into a production line.

steps.title: Decomposition of Features
steps.description: Break your massive feature list into tasks smaller than 4 hours of work. This prevents the 'overwhelmed' state that leads to project abandonment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this feature list and decompose it into atomic, sequential tasks that can be completed in under 4 hours each. Output as a structured JSON roadmap.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Break down requirements.md into a sequence of atomic tickets'

steps.title: Establish the Skeleton
steps.description: Generate the project structure before writing business logic. This ensures your architecture is sound and scalable from the first line.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready folder structure and boilerplate for a [Type] application. Include best practices for folder separation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --init 'Initialize project scaffolding based on industry standards'

steps.title: Rapid Iteration Cycles
steps.description: Use Claude Code to handle boilerplate implementation so you can focus on high-level business logic and client-facing features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the boilerplate for this API route, ensuring error handling and input validation are included.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --code 'Implement authentication middleware with JWT support'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Integrate tests early. A project that is 'finished' but broken is useless. Testing as you go keeps the codebase clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the newly created modules to ensure full coverage and regression protection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --test 'Generate and run unit tests for the auth module'

steps.title: Final Review and Polish
steps.description: Perform a final audit to ensure the code matches the initial scope, avoiding unnecessary gold-plating that delays delivery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase against the original project requirements and identify any scope creep or unfinished logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --audit 'Compare code against requirements.md and flag discrepancies'

pro_tips: Never write code without a 'Definition of Done' for every ticket; it prevents the infinite loop of tweaks.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation as you build, not after; it creates a self-documenting project history.
Adopt a 'Ship-First, Perfect-Later' mindset; optimize for client delivery, then refactor during maintenance cycles.
Keep a 'Parking Lot' document for cool ideas that aren't in the current scope to prevent feature creep.
Automate your deployment pipeline early; seeing a live URL is the best motivation to finish a project.
content.next_steps: After completing this checklist, integrate your workflow into a CI/CD pipeline and publish your project to a staging environment to get immediate feedback from your client.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting half-finished code sit on your hard drive. Master the workflow that turns ideas into shipped products. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
